free_text: Elastic Scattering of Low-Energy Deuterons from 60Ni
and 114Cd and the Effect of the Deuteron Electric
The detector used was a solid-state surface
barrier counter, and the overall resolution at the
Excitation functions were obtained for both
targets over the complete energy range for both
elastic and inelastic scattering, taking data points
every 250 keV, for theta lab = 60 and 110 degrees. No
structure was observed in any of these excitation
The error in the subtraction was estimated to
be within 1-5%. The absolute error assigned to the
measured cross sections depended primarily on the
measured target thicknesses, the detector solid angle
measurement and the beam integration measurements,
and was estimated to be less than 10%. From the
excitation curves the relative cross sections at the
different energies are estimated to be accurate to 5%.
Statistical errors in the numbers of counts in the
inelastic peaks were less than 3%, except for the
most forward angles at the lower energies.
